paper_title: DragMesh-2: Physically Plausible Dexterous Hand-Object Interaction with Articulated Objects

traceability_manifest.claim: Articulated objects cannot be directly controlled; their motion must emerge through sustained hand–object contact

location.text: The policy controls only the hand. The object joint has no action channel, so the target part can move only through hand–handle contact.

evidence.detail: This is the core task formulation of DragMesh-2. The target joint has no action channel; only 51-DoF hand is controlled.

supporting_details: Hand: 51-DoF SMPL-X (6 wrist DoFs + 45 finger joints)
Observation: No RGB, depth, point clouds, force, or tactile signals
Action: 51-dimensional increment to hand PD target, clipped to joint limits

traceability_manifest.claim: Geometric trajectory replay or open-loop execution does not model the contact dynamics required to move the articulated part

location.text: Trajectory tracking reaches 1.00 at ×1 on all objects but drops to 0.71 at ×2 and ×4

supporting_details: 7 GAPartNet objects, 20 episodes each
Deterministic execution only (open-loop)

traceability_manifest.claim: Policies trained only for task completion under fixed dynamics can overfit nominal contact loads, especially without tactile or force feedback

location.text: As training extends from 150 to 500 epochs, nominal success rises from 0.90 to 1.00, yet strong-damping success collapses from 0.55 to 0.10

evidence.detail: Training-length study on a single object: longer training buys nominal success but drives policy into saturated low-robustness regime

supporting_details: clip099: fraction of rollout steps with max action magnitude > 0.99
checkpoint selection by OOD robustness, not training reward

traceability_manifest.claim: PICA injects computationally observable physical signals into policy learning without tactile or force feedback

evidence.detail: PICA augments PPO with contact-aware reward terms, causal-window auxiliary supervision, damping randomization, and temporal contact-response modeling

supporting_details: Reward: r_task + r_dist + r_act + r_time + r_detach + r_success + r_bound + r_contact
Auxiliary: 4-target contact response prediction (object motion, palm distance, detachment, tracking stress)
Damping randomization during training
GLA temporal encoder for contact-history features

traceability_manifest.claim: DragMesh-2 achieves stronger robustness under contact-load variation than compared methods

evidence.detail: PICA attains the highest mean success in all six mode × damping settings across 7 GAPartNet objects

supporting_details: 7 objects from GAPartNet
20 episodes per (method, object, damping, mode) cell
Damping conditions: ×1 (nominal), ×2 (mild), ×4 (OOD)

traceability_manifest.claim: PICA retains the highest absolute success under strong damping

location.text: 0.56 deterministic at ×4 versus 0.27 for state-only PPO and 0.09 for Transformer-PPO

evidence.detail: At ×4 deterministic: PICA=0.56, State-only PPO=0.27, Transformer-PPO=0.09; PICA is >2x the next best learned method

supporting_details: All learned methods compared under identical conditions
20 episodes per cell

traceability_manifest.claim: Richer temporal encoders alone do not close the gap to PICA

location.text: GRU (0.30), Transformer (0.09), and all lag PICA by at least 0.13 at ×4

evidence.detail: At ×4 deterministic: GRU-PPO=0.30, Transformer-PPO=0.09, GLA-only=0.36 vs PICA=0.56

supporting_details: Transformer-PPO actually performs worst — richer architecture alone not sufficient
Gap: PICA ≥ GRU+0.26, ≥ Transformer+0.47 at ×4

traceability_manifest.claim: The win is the combination of physical signals with temporal contact-response modeling, not the temporal encoder by itself

location.text: w/o PICA (GLA only): 0.65→0.36; w/o GLA (PICA only): 0.75→0.43; Full PICA: 0.89→0.56

evidence.detail: Ablation study isolating physical signals vs temporal encoder. Full model exceeds either component by ≥0.13 at ×4

supporting_details: Physical signals contribute more under nominal damping
Temporal encoder helps more under stochastic mid-damping
Components are complementary, not redundant

traceability_manifest.claim: Loss of contact at OOD damping is due to saturated action, not policy failure to track the desired motion

location.text: clip099 climbs toward 1.0 as strong-damping success collapses

evidence.detail: clip099 (action saturation proxy) increases from 0.90 to 0.99 as ×4 success drops from 0.55 to 0.10 across training epochs

supporting_details: clip099: max action magnitude > 0.99
detach_proxy: additional contact diagnostic

traceability_manifest.claim: Physical signals contribute more under nominal damping, temporal encoder helps more under stochastic mid-damping

location.text: The components help along different axes

evidence.detail: w/o GLA at ×1: 0.75 > w/o PICA at ×1: 0.65 (physical signals dominate at nominal); w/o PICA under stochastic mid-damping shows relative improvement

supporting_details: Based on ablation study in Appendix C.8
Claimed finding from ablation comparison

traceability_manifest.claim: PICA components are complementary rather than redundant

location.text: Full model exceeds either component by ≥0.13 at ×4

evidence.detail: Full PICA (0.56 at ×4) > max(w/o PICA=0.36, w/o GLA=0.43) by 0.13

supporting_details: Held across all damping levels and execution modes

traceability_manifest.claim: No single policy dominates every object instance

location.text: per-object results remain heterogeneous, so no single policy dominates every instance

evidence.detail: Per-object results vary substantially; PICA fails on some objects even at ×1 (e.g., 45661 StorageFurniture door: 0.85 at ×1)

supporting_details: 7 objects from GAPartNet
Different categories: StorageFurniture, Dishwasher, Microwave, etc.

traceability_manifest.claim: A parallel-jaw primitive cannot substitute for dexterous contact control even with GT part pose

location.text: Parallel-jaw only 0.14 mean success across all objects and dampings

evidence.detail: GT-part-pose parallel-jaw succeeds on only 1/7 objects (0.14 mean), damping-invariant

supporting_details: Non-learned baseline with ground-truth part pose information
Succeeds only on the simplest geometry instance

traceability_manifest.claim: Extended fine-tuning and damping-range expansion do not yield stable additional OOD gains

location.text: Robustness under strong contact load requires richer contact interfaces rather than longer optimization

evidence.detail: Appendix reports extended fine-tuning and wider damping distribution both fail to improve OOD robustness meaningfully

supporting_details: Detailed in Appendix C.4 (Limits of Extended Fine-Tuning)
Appendix C.5 (Limits of Damping-Distribution Expansion)

traceability_manifest.claim: Longer training buys nominal success by driving the policy into a saturated, low-robustness regime

location.text: Longer training therefore buys nominal success by driving the policy into a saturated, low-robustness regime

supporting_details: Directly motivates reporting OOD damping and saturation metrics
Motivates checkpoint selection by OOD robustness rather than training reward

traceability_manifest.claim: Nominal success does not guarantee stable contact behavior

location.text: Success under nominal dynamics does not necessarily imply stable contact behavior

evidence.detail: This is a central thesis of the paper, supported by multiple experiments including the training-length study and OOD damping evaluation

supporting_details: Supported by C1.3 (table 4) and C2.2 (×4 performance)

traceability_manifest.claim: The dataset contains 277 trajectories over 7 GAPartNet categories

evidence.detail: 277 heuristic trajectories across categories: StorageFurniture (256), TrashCan (7), Dishwasher (5), Refrigerator (4), Oven (3), Microwave (1), TableObject (1)

supporting_details: Generated heuristicially from GAPartNet geometry, without learning
Four phases: approach → grasp → drag → release

traceability_manifest.claim: The dataset is generated heuristicially, without learning, directly from GAPartNet geometry

location.text: The dataset is generated heuristicially, without learning, directly from GAPartNet geometry

evidence.detail: Geometry-guided procedure reads part, handle, and joint-mobility annotations with an SMPL-X hand model and emits phased interaction trajectory

supporting_details: Uses geometric heuristics (not learning)
Provides expert grasp state initialization, motion-scale normalization, and tracking reference
